skill.name: ai-avatar-video
skill.description: Create AI avatar and talking head videos using the inference.sh CLI. Generate virtual presenters, UGC content, and lipsync videos from portrait images and text scripts.
skill.summary: Generate AI avatar videos, talking head clips, and lipsync content via the inference.sh belt CLI. Supports P-Video-Avatar, OmniHuman, Fabric, and PixVerse models.
skill.author: halt-catch-fire

security_audit.summary: The skill is a documentation file describing how to use the inference.sh CLI (belt) for AI avatar video generation. All 56 static findings are false positives: the 'external_commands' detections are bash code blocks showing legitimate belt CLI usage examples, not actual code execution. The 'network' findings are documentation URLs and placeholder example links. No malicious patterns, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempts were found.

low_findings.title: External CLI tool usage documented
low_findings.description: The skill documents usage of the 'belt' CLI tool from inference.sh for AI video generation. This is the intended functionality, not a security risk. Users must install and authenticate with belt to use these features.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct review of SKILL.md confirms all flagged commands are documentation examples for the legitimate belt CLI tool. No actual executable code exists in this skill file.

low_findings.title: Network URLs in documentation
low_findings.description: The skill contains URLs to documentation pages and example placeholder URLs. These are legitimate references to the inference.sh platform and example media files, not data exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs point to inference.sh documentation or are placeholder examples (https://portrait.jpg). No suspicious external endpoints detected.

content.user_title: Create AI Avatar Videos with Simple CLI Commands
content.value_statement: Creating professional AI avatar videos requires complex pipelines with multiple AI models. This skill simplifies the process by providing ready-to-use CLI commands for generating talking head videos, lipsync content, and virtual presenters.
actual_capabilities: Generate AI avatar videos from portrait images and text scripts using P-Video-Avatar model
Create talking head videos with built-in text-to-speech in 10 languages
Produce lipsync videos from existing audio files using Fabric or PixVerse models
Generate multi-character avatar videos with OmniHuman 1.5
Create UGC-style content with voice and video prompt controls for tone and body language
Dub existing videos into other languages using transcription, translation, and lipsync workflows
limitations: Requires installation and authentication of the inference.sh belt CLI tool
Video generation costs vary by model, ranging from $0.025 to $0.16 per second
Output quality depends on input portrait image quality and lighting conditions
Some models lack built-in TTS, requiring separate audio generation steps

use_cases.title: Marketing Teams Creating UGC Ads
use_cases.description: Generate authentic-looking UGC-style video ads with AI presenters for social media campaigns at scale.

use_cases.title: Content Creators Building Virtual Influencers
use_cases.description: Create consistent virtual influencer content with custom avatars for ongoing social media presence.

use_cases.title: Educators Producing Course Videos
use_cases.description: Generate explainer videos and course content with AI presenters in multiple languages.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Avatar Video with TTS
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an AI avatar video using the recommended P-Video-Avatar model. Use my portrait image at [IMAGE_URL] and have the avatar say '[YOUR_SCRIPT]' with the [VOICE_NAME] voice in [LANGUAGE].

prompt_templates.title: UGC-Style Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a casual UGC-style video using P-Video-Avatar. Portrait: [IMAGE_URL]. Script: '[CASUAL_SCRIPT]'. Voice: [VOICE]. Voice prompt: 'Excited, casual, authentic tone like talking to a friend'. Video prompt: 'Person talking casually to camera in their room, natural gestures'. Resolution: 1080p.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Language Video Dubbing
prompt_templates.prompt: Dub my video at [VIDEO_URL] into [TARGET_LANGUAGE]. Steps: 1) Transcribe with fast-whisper-large-v3, 2) Translate the text, 3) Generate new speech with kokoro-tts, 4) Apply lipsync with latentsync-1-6.

prompt_templates.title: Batch UGC Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate 3 UGC videos with different presenters using a for loop. Use voices: [VOICE_1], [VOICE_2], [VOICE_3]. Same portrait: [IMAGE_URL]. Same script: '[TESTIMONIAL_SCRIPT]'. Resolution: 1080p.

output_examples.input: Portrait URL and script: 'Welcome to our product demo. Today I will show you three key features.'
output_examples.output: AI-generated video of the portrait person speaking the script with natural lip movements and expressions, available in 720p or 1080p resolution.

output_examples.input: Request to dub a video into Spanish using the dubbing workflow.
output_examples.output: Original video with new Spanish audio track and synchronized lip movements. Process includes transcription, translation, TTS generation, and lipsync application.

output_examples.input: Batch generation request for 3 different presenters with the same testimonial script.
output_examples.output: Three separate video files, each featuring a different voice persona delivering the same script with authentic, casual delivery style.

best_practices: Use high-quality portrait photos with front-facing pose and good lighting for best results
Start with P-Video-Avatar for most use cases since it is 18x faster and 6x cheaper than alternatives
Generate portraits in 9:16 aspect ratio for vertical social media videos
Use the built-in TTS feature of P-Video-Avatar to simplify your workflow and reduce costs
anti_patterns: Do not use low-quality or side-profile portrait images as they produce poor avatar results
Do not use models without built-in TTS when P-Video-Avatar can handle your use case
Do not skip audio quality checks, as background noise degrades lipsync accuracy

faq.question: Which AI model should I use for avatar videos?
faq.answer: P-Video-Avatar is recommended for most use cases. It is 18x faster and 6x cheaper than alternatives, with built-in TTS, dynamic backgrounds, and 1080p support.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to install the inference.sh belt CLI tool and authenticate with belt login before running the avatar generation commands.

faq.question: How much does it cost to generate avatar videos?
faq.answer: Costs vary by model. P-Video-Avatar costs $0.025 per second. OmniHuman costs $0.16 per second, and Fabric costs $0.14 per second.

faq.question: Can I create videos in languages other than English?
faq.answer: Yes. P-Video-Avatar supports 10 languages with built-in TTS. For other models, you can use a separate TTS step with kokoro-tts or inworld-tts-2.

faq.question: How do I dub an existing video into another language?
faq.answer: Use the dubbing workflow: transcribe with fast-whisper, translate the text, generate new speech with TTS, then apply lipsync with latentsync-1-6.

faq.question: Can I generate multiple videos with different presenters?
faq.answer: Yes. Use a bash for loop to iterate through different voices and generate multiple videos with the same portrait and script.
